Awesome.

and she, yeah, OK, cool,
yeah, that's like the biggest thing.

But essentially, a little bit about us, we've been around for the last 5 years and so we have been pulling um like rent comp data, just overall market data from 100.
publicly available sources all the way down to the unit level.

Um, so within our platform, there's actually like 3 different functionalities, um, one of which is for like the day to day operations where you get a live data feed into not only your assets, but all the comps are tracking for each and um that way you can see where your floor plans and units are positioned within like your given market.
Um, second functionality is more towards like acquisitions, new development, but you can spin up these custom market surveys, um, in a matter of seconds.
All you need is the asset name or the address, and then our system will, will spit out as many of these market surveys as you'd like for any asset you, you don't own or manage.
And then the last one is going to be all market submarket level data.
So, like, for example, I'm in Scottsdale, so if you wanted to take a look at like the broader like Phoenix market.
You could pull that up, you'd get uh analysis for that entire market.
Um, you'd also have 5 years of our historical database all the way down to the the unit level to to take a look at for seasonality and trends.
Um, and again, you could drill down to that market data at the at the market level submarket, zip code, neighborhood, um, all that good stuff.
So there's a It used to just be like a market survey tool.
Now it's like an overall like market data like product for just multiple arms within all of these property management companies.

I think we could help your investment team, um, and, and so on and so forth.

OK.
Let me share my screen.
I'll walk you through it.

Research would be the ability to spend these custom market surveys up and explore all markets of market level data.

All the comps set across the top here.

Um, that's user specific, so if you like to show certain metrics that that won't affect how others see it when they log in.
Um, and then I think something that's very nice here is I feel like comps are, are always changing whether there's like new lease ups or, you know, new takers or anything like that.
So if you'll notice here in the top right, there's an edit comps button.
What I like about our platform is you're in complete control of your compet uh the entire time, and you can change it as often as you'd like.
So if you click on edit comps.

The white pin or comps in the set, and if you hover over it, we'll give you more information on it.
If you want to remove this comp, you can just click remove.
The black pin are going to be assets that our system is flagged in the area for you to take a look at.
It's as simple as hovering over and clicking add.

Um, a more like analytical approach would be the left hand side here, but we will actually, uh, give all these assets what we call a comp score, and we'll list it out from high to low, and that is based off of distance to your property, the year built or renovation date, the total number of units in the asset, and then we match rents and we match amenities, and we spit out these scores, and again, you can click the extra move or click the add to add the comp.
So we make it super easy for you.

Um, another thing is, if, if you don't want to scroll through the list or you don't want to take a look at the map, you could just type in the name of the address and it will, it will automatically add it along with the five years of uh historicals as well.
So Um, so I'll walk you through the market survey.
There's 4 sections.

You had asked about that.
The nice thing too is with any of these metrics, since this is all coming from publicly available sources, um, you can drill down and track all of these.

Um, so we get extremely granular here and it's nice if you click into any of the floor plan names or the individual units, we'll show you the price history for it.

So for example, any sort of gap in the data means that there was no uh whatever floor plan this is, none available, but if it is available, we'll show you both the gross net effective and the concession attached to it over that given time frame.
So that's at the floor plan, same thing for the uh the unit level history.
So we'll give you that as well.

sorry, would you be able to see the concession details even if they didn't have an active concession at that time?
